The fact that the checking account balance is negative, because it reflects active financial distress, is known in the literature as a signal that increases credit risk, and the model also identifies it as a factor that increases risk.[1]
The fact that the loan duration is extended, because extended repayment horizons amplify lender exposure to borrower financial deterioration, is known in the literature as a signal that increases credit risk, and the model also identifies it as a factor that increases risk.[2]
The fact that there is no checking account, due to the complete absence of a checking account correlating with a more favorable credit profile, is known in the literature as a signal that decreases credit risk, but the model instead identifies it as a factor that increases risk.[3]
